programmes.show_description: More cutting-edge stand-up from the iconic theatre in London. The magnificent Zoe Lyons hosts and welcomes comedians Ria Lina and Tom Ward to the stage.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Richard Ayoade is host as comedian Jack Dee and broadcaster Kelly Cates join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Lawn Signs: Settle in for another hilarious episode of the multi-award-winning sitcom. Some of Moira's lawn signs disappear, and David and Alexis work to get David's boss out of trouble.
Happy Anniversary: The last episode in the second series of the sitcom. Johnny and Moira see a couple they knew in a past life, and Alexis and David go to Mutt's annual barn party.
Opening Night: The kick-off for the third series of the hit comedy from across the pond. Johnny searches for a new purpose as Moira faces the realities of politics.
The Throuple: Another edition of the down-to-earth sitcom. David and Stevie try to come to terms with their new relationship, and Alexis and Moira plan a mother-daughter lunch.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
War: The bikers explore how the British have still managed to create remarkable dishes, even in times of war, trying their hand at rabbit stew and carrot cake.
An elderly couple arrives from Mexico with five suitcases but no clothes, and a truck carrying garden furniture has something extra in the load.
Officers pull over a man from Mexico who is making a rare journey to the US in a new car and a pedestrian gets more than he bargains for when he is mistaken for a wanted man.
Documentary series. The Ghan train takes 288 passengers across Australia, and there is trouble on the tracks as a vital rail line is in urgent need of repair.
Rick Stein presents a series exploring the universal passion for food. In this edition, Rick finds out how baguettes have helped to reform a former drug addict.
Rick Stein presents a series exploring the passion provoked by food. In this edition, Rick supplies low-fat diet ideas to the Bradford Bulls and tries Yugoslav salami.
Officers prepare to transfer two members of a cartel. In Laredo, a cop from Caracas, Venezuela, pleads his case for asylum in the United States.
A refugee from Venezuela tells officers how she paid human traffickers to smuggle her to the Mexico-US border. In Laredo, an American couple are caught smuggling methamphetamine.
Guatemala: Joe Swash and Seann Walsh are no strangers to jungles after appearing on I'm A Celebrity..., but neither of them has sought an ancient Mayan temple in Guatemala before.
Clarkson gets chased by the British Army in some of their deadliest new toys, while Hammond and May race the Royal Mail - no jokes about slow deliveries, please. Usain Bolt guests.
Turbo-charged motoring show with Clarkson and the team. Driving three 1,500-pound rear-wheel drive coupes, the boys end up in a terrifying French ice race. Sienna Miller guests.
War: The bikers explore how the British have still managed to create remarkable dishes, even in times of war, trying their hand at rabbit stew and carrot cake.
Rick Stein presents a series exploring the universal passion for food. In this edition, Rick finds out how baguettes have helped to reform a former drug addict.
Rick Stein presents a series exploring the passion provoked by food. In this edition, Rick supplies low-fat diet ideas to the Bradford Bulls and tries Yugoslav salami.
More Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack, and David Mitchell return for a further episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show.
Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Dr Ranj Singh and Meera Syal enter Richard's fortress of quiz to test their general knowledge skills, but who will come out on top this time?
Entering Richard's home of general knowledge in this edition of the smash-hit quiz show are Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen.

programmes.show_name: New: Will & Ralf Should Know Better
programmes.show_description: Fears: It's time for Will and Ralf to face their fears, from dangling on a trapeze and tackling fatherhood to the ultimate horror of performing stand-up in front of a Glasgow crowd.
Maths: Stephen Fry dispels the factoids that don't quite add up in the panel show that's tougher than algebra. With Sandi Toksvig, Aisling Bea, Susan Calman and Alan Davies.
I've Been a Bit Ill: Paul Sinha, Lou Sanders and Iain Stirling become one while Greg Davies watches on, ready to issue his judgements. Sian Gibson seeks to redefine the definition of bounce.
Mockers Chris Addison, Hugh Dennis and Andy Hamilton find plenty to ridicule in the week's news with the help of Gary Delaney, Miles Jupp and Nathan Caton. Dara O Briain hosts.
Brazen bluffing with Rob Brydon, David Mitchell and Lee Mack in the very witty panel show. Also telling pork pies are Rhod Gilbert, Vernon Kay, Dara O Briain and Denise Van Outen.
More Unseen Bits: Rob Brydon, Lee Mack, and David Mitchell return for a further episode of previously unseen material from the hit comedy panel show.
Football, Fascists & The Frontline: Jamali meets with Azov, a far-right militant group in Ukraine that has gained legitimacy despite its neo-Nazi roots.
Sweden's Far Right Youth: Jamali embeds himself in the Nordic Youth - a group who patrol Sweden's streets hunting refugees - to try to understand their response to a migration crisis.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Picnics: The bikers explore our national love of picnics, celebrating classics such as the Cornish pasty and the Scotch egg, and baking a luxurious Dundee cake.
Officers in Brownsville process an American citizen who crossed over the Rio Grande 20 years ago - and discover the reason he didn't want to come back.
A Cuban woman crosses the bridge in the hope of being granted political asylum in America, while officers in Laredo stop three students carrying something unusual in their car.
Documentary series. Timetables are tight as a major rail line is closed for repairs, and train spotters hunt for a locomotive that has been dubbed the Great White Shark.
Rick Stein explores people's passion for food - here he visits the people behind a Bristol initiative which helps low-income families cook healthy and affordable food.
Rick Stein presents a series exploring people's passion for food. In this edition, he meets a former schoolteacher who lives and eats like a Victorian.
A family from Venezuela plead with officers to enter America. In Progreso, a suspected drug dealer is caught smuggling pills in his underpants.
More than 100 boxes full of drugs are discovered inside an unlikely vehicle at the World Trade Bridge. In Progreso, a suspected gang member attempts to cross into the US.
Sri Lanka: Former Soccer Saturday buddies Jeff Stelling and Chris Kamara reunite for an epic journey across Sri Lanka where they reaffirm their friendship along the way.
The motoring show that really packs a punch. The boys each attempt to buy a pre-1982 car at auction before heading to a classic car rally. AC/DC's Brian Johnson guests.
Jeremy Clarkson and James May fight while attempting to create a memorable TV advert for Volkswagen! The Cool Wall returns, while US chat show host Jay Leno is the boys' guest.
Picnics: The bikers explore our national love of picnics, celebrating classics such as the Cornish pasty and the Scotch egg, and baking a luxurious Dundee cake.
Rick Stein explores people's passion for food - here he visits the people behind a Bristol initiative which helps low-income families cook healthy and affordable food.
Rick Stein presents a series exploring people's passion for food. In this edition, he meets a former schoolteacher who lives and eats like a Victorian.
At Christmas: Joining Rob Brydon, Lee Mack and David Mitchell for this special Christmas episode are Liz Bonnin, Stephen Merchant, Sharon Osbourne and Dr Ranj Singh.
Steve Backshall, Catherine Bohart, Dr Ranj Singh and Meera Syal enter Richard's fortress of quiz to test their general knowledge skills, but who will come out on top this time?
Entering Richard's home of general knowledge in this edition of the smash-hit quiz show are Angela Barnes, Melvin Odoom, Greg Rutherford and Denise Van Outen.
Marriage and Mating: The stimulating panel show that weds comedy and knowledge. Stephen Fry enlightens Jo Brand, Greg Davies, Bill Bailey and Alan Davies on the joys of sex.

programmes.show_name: Have I Got a Bit More News for You
programmes.show_description: Roy Wood Jnr is host as writer and broadcaster Richard Osman and comedian Roisin Conaty join team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop to delve into the news.
Clumpy Swayey Clumsy Man: Lou Sanders chews on some stationery, Sian Gibson is crushed by a caravan, Paul Sinha wrecks a camera, Joe Thomas gets cross and Iain Stirling waves a stick about.
